Skip to content

Administrative Appeals Board's Decisions

Administrative Appeals Board's Decisions

The Administrative Appeals Board (“AAB”) hears and determines appeals lodged against PCPD’s enforcement decisions. AAB may confirm, vary or reverse PCPD’s decisions. It has given PCPD its permission to publish on PCPD website its decisions delivered after open hearings. The general practice of PCPD is to upload AAB’s decisions on an “as is” basis. Decisions delivered by AAB after private hearings are not published.

 

The purpose of publishing AAB’s decisions on PCPD website is primarily to promote awareness and understanding of, and compliance with, the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance. Use of any personal data contained in AAB’s decisions for any other purpose may constitute a breach of the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance.

The copyright in the decisions is owned by the Administrative Appeals Board ("AAB"). The decisions are published on PCPD's website with the permission of the AAB. AAB accepts no liability or responsibility for the accuracy or completeness of any of the decisions being published on PCPD's website.

The decisions on this website are for reference only. Whilst PCPD endeavours to ensure the accuracy of information on this site, no express or implied warranty is given by the PCPD as to the accuracy of information.
